content: Notes from @Aakash: Overall: Maybe+ EO: 6-7 | FMF: 5-6 | Thesis: 6 | Price: ? Spikey: ~1 (need refs) - Thesis is a compelling one + have built early momentum - Can see a strong growth mindset and they are building in a deep familiarity space - Would likely have to be a services + product play ideally – for which they seem like an incomplete team - Not SaaS native Next Steps: Push them on GTM thinking with Kuldeep, Rohit and other SaaS folks. Notes Asad Abrar - Drizz.dev Born in Patna Raised by Mother , father used to work in Saudi (used to come once in 2 years) IT KGP 5 years | Order of merit in technology | graduated in 2014 After college got off campus placement @ Baker Hughes 1 year worked at Baker Hughes Then started a company similar to go mechanic Why did you start then ? Had nothing to lose. Was seeing value creation in startups from batch mates Manager at baker hughes was co-founder at the first venture | total 4 co-fos “ You seem like tech inclined how come an ops heavy biz?” That time tech enabled cos we’re all around. Tech to streamlines believer Ran for 1.5 years there | got to monhly 10L GMV Realised problem was two fold : - Old cars for repair used to turn up ..only post service centre cycle was done for the owner - Repair at doorstep was becoming a challenge in an asset light model - Logisitcs of getting parts to doorstep became a challenge - Pivoted to b2b | they were partners for Ola - Realised its a very linear scaling biz and momentum was low - There were a...

Notes:: Overall – Maybe + for DeVC Spikey = 0.5 (closer to 1) 30/40 - FMF 7 | EO 7 | Thesis 8 | Price 8 Overall, + Quite thoughtful on how to build (shows up in product formulation, pricing focus, investment in manufacturing) + Surrounding himself with people (2 food scientists in house / ITC factory folks are helping to scale up production / Devendra Chawla, Nature’s Basket head is working closely and has invested) + Clear articulation on what he wants to focus on and learning from other food brands + Thoughtful about fund raise (doesn’t want to raise too much as not needed, wants to get thoughtful partners on board) ? Didn’t speak to him about scale of ambition of journey over next few years Next Steps : Connect him to Revant (to discuss team building for brand business) and Chakri (on online and offline distribution / category building learnings). Also, get feedback and BRCs at the right time. DeVC’s edge here will be getting the right people to help him think through the larger questions. - - Fund Raise - Has closed 12cr at 75cr pre with Prath. Had a TS (?) from Fireside for 16cr but couldn’t get his angels out (he has ~25 through Mumbai angels + RUV on cap table). Stopped taking calls from other VCs for now – in Due D - Total raised before this 5.5cr in ’22 and 3cr in ’23 (Java doubling down) - 3 weeks since Due D started, thinks should be done with SHA as well by end October Business + Grown 0.5cr, 5, 15 last year and this year targeting 25-30 + L3M 1.6 (J), 1.8 (J), 3....

(1) Founders and Key Team: Abhinav Kankane – Title not disclosed [Inference] Career: INSEAD, IIT-Madras; ex-Zalando, P&G, UC Focus: Business, GTM, Partnerships Functional responsibility: GTM & Partnerships Equity & commitment: Not disclosed Relevance: Strong cross-border business and partnerships background relevant for scaling GTM. Siddharth Shrivastava – Title not disclosed [Inference] Career: IIT-Madras; ex-WinZO, Sprinklr Focus: Product, Content, Pedagogy Functional responsibility: Product, content, pedagogy Equity & commitment: Not disclosed Relevance: Deep product and pedagogy experience fits Eduro’s core learning app. Kavish Shah – Title not disclosed [Inference] Career: MS @ UPenn, IIT-Madras; ex-Vanguard Focus: Technology & Innovation Functional responsibility: Tech leadership Equity & commitment: Not disclosed Relevance: Technical rigor and innovation background supportive for AI-driven product. Adwait Sudersan – Title not disclosed [Inference] Career: IIT-Delhi; ex-WinZO; ex-founder Focus: Technology & Innovation Functional responsibility: Technology & Innovation Equity & commitment: Not disclosed Relevance: Founder-level technical background and prior startup experience.
(2) Product and Technology: Product stage: [Inference] Not disclosed Core functionality & tech stack: Interactive Learning Canvas with curriculum-aligned generative components; Central Memory Layer for pace, mastery, misconceptions; Motivational Loops for daily engagement; AI components (LLMs), TT...

Inbound via Chandrika (Quivly) - 2026-02-28: Source: Chandrika Maheshwari (Quivly) forwarded to Cash on Feb 28, 2026. Chandrika says she's been "super impressed with the product" - Quivly is a design partner. PITCH (from Shagun's email Feb 27): "Engram is the brand brain for fast-scaling AI companies. As a company grows, brand and marketing context (how you position, how you respond to the market, what sounds like you) becomes impossible to transfer at scale. The faster you grow, the worse it gets. Engram makes brand intelligence institutional so it compounds as you scale instead of fragmenting. The system learns your taste, your context, and your decision-making, and turns it into something your whole team can use." STATUS: Live with design partners and a few early paying customers, all Seed-to-Series A AI companies. Planning to raise a pre-seed soon. FOUNDERS: 1. Shagun Ohri (CEO) - shagun@engramhq.com - 2x founder, 14+ years in brand and marketing - Most recently Director of Marketing at Together Fund working with Series A AI companies - Rahul's concern: "background of hopping around quite a bit" - suggested checking with Subham at Together Fund 2. Siddhant Singh (CTO) - siddhant@engramhq.com - Previously built a generative media platform for enterprise (Volkswagen, J&J) - Scaled Flipkart Ads to $1B ARR - PRIOR Z47 HISTORY: Affinity shows email exchanges and meeting in Feb 2024 (last email: Feb 20, last event: Feb 8). Personal email: siddhant0204@gmail.com OPEN ITEMS: - Ca...

About the Company: - ExtraaEdge provides a full-stack marketing and sales SaaS platform for the education industry, leveraging predictive analytics, automation, and AI-driven tools to improve student acquisition and recruitment processes. - They are targeting educational institutions such as universities, test prep organizations, overseas study consultants, and coaching centers. - Annual Recurring Revenue (ARR): $2M (2023-24) and has grown 3.2x ARR since 2022. - They have 276 global education brands, including universities and mid-market players with the following split - Enterprise (23%), Mid-Market (15%), SMB (62%). - The core offerings are 1) predictive enrolment engine with 83% accuracy using proprietary LLMs and ML models, 2) automated CRM, lead nurturing, ROI trackers, and centralized communication tools, 3) GenAI-powered co-pilots for sales teams and prospective students to enhance conversion rates - They are well reviewed and testimonials indicate improved application rates by 22% and increased conversions for remote counseling teams by 18%, making it a trusted partner in education recruitment. - They are currently selling in MENA, UK, Europe and India Founders - Abhishek Ballabh (CEO) - Data Science and B2B SaaS expert with a background in AI and ML. - ExtraaEdge – ’18 - now - OnDeck – Founder Fellowship ’22 - SaaSBoomi – SGx W20 - Instructional Design Head – MindTickle ’13-15 - Software Architech – HSBC ‘09-13 - Sushil Mundada (COO) - 15+ years in product engineerin...

AI notes:: Anirudh, founder of Faff, presented their personal assistant service to potential investors from DVC. Faff aims to democratize access to personal assistants through a combination of human operators and AI automation. Founder Background - Anirudh graduated from IIT Bombay in 2022 (Chemical Engineering) - Worked at Facebook AI Research (FAIR) in London on reducing AI hallucinations - Met co-founder Het Patel at an Entrepreneur First hackathon - Raised $125K from Entrepreneur First in December Business Model & Metrics - Personal assistant service targeting tasks not covered by specialized apps - Users send requests via WhatsApp to a Relationship Manager who assigns tasks to "Faffers" - 182 current users paying ₹2,000/month - Top users average 5 tasks per week - Target demographic: 25-35 year olds with ~₹40 LPA income - Most common tasks: digital tasks (web check-ins, Uber booking), finding local services, and sourcing local products Operations & Economics - Relationship Manager assigns tasks to Faffers (task executors) - Currently paying Faffers ₹50K/month, planning to reduce to ₹30K with better SOPs - Each Faffer currently handles ~12 tasks/day - Negative unit economics with ₹2K gross margin burn per customer - Payments handled through user wallet or direct payment by users Automation Strategy - 6140% of voice workflow already automated (restaurant reservations, pharmacy calls) -  4114/*-92w34r5t678Goal to increase Faffer productivity from 12 to 50+ tasks per day - C...

AI notes: Jigar, a former Goldman Sachs risk analyst, presented his startup which is building an AI-powered analytics platform for asset managers. The meeting covered product strategy, market positioning, and fundraising plans. Background and Experience - Worked at Goldman Sachs (2016-2020) in Risk Informatics team - Used machine learning and AI for firm-wide risk management - Developed systems to analyze 50 scenarios across 2 million positions - Previously worked in market risk for Asia Special Situations Group - Focused on ensuring no FX or interest rate risk in emerging markets deals Product Vision - Creating AI-powered risk and factor analysis for fundamental equity investors - Key workflow integration: morning portfolio review meetings - Generates reports analyzing what moved the portfolio yesterday - Creates customized factor models with real-world explanations - Not positioning as "selling alpha generation" (strategic approach) - Integrating with data sources including Polymarket, Google News, and Alpha Vantage Target Market - Fundamental asset managers with under $100B AUM - Focus on firms without large quant teams (80% of market currently underserved) - Initial beachhead: firms with 2-3 quant people who need supplemental capabilities - Already working with design partners like Marcellus, Arga, GCM, and Grosvenor Business Potential - Current market: similar services generate $800M revenue with only 10% AUM penetration - Aladdin (competitor) generates $1B in revenue wi...
